title_en: Al-Hidayah Sharh Bidayat al-Mubtadi: The Gateway Commentary
content_en:
Al-Hidayah fi Sharh Bidayat al-Mubtadi (The Guidance: A Commentary on The Beginning for the Student) by Burhan ad-Din Ali ibn Abi Bakr al-Marghinani (511–593 AH / 1135–1197 CE) is the most authoritative and widely used text in the Hanafi school. Written as a commentary on al-Marghinani's own primer Bidayat al-Mubtadi, Al-Hidayah provides the explanations, evidential basis, and legal reasoning that make the primer's concise rulings intelligible and applicable.

The relationship between Al-Hidayah and Bidayat al-Mubtadi is unique in the Islamic legal tradition: the same scholar wrote both the primer and its commentary, meaning that the commentary reflects an authoritative interpretation of the original text rather than a later scholar's reading of another's work. Al-Marghinani knew exactly what he intended by each ruling in Bidayat al-Mubtadi and explains it in Al-Hidayah with the authority of the original author.

Al-Hidayah's organization follows Bidayat al-Mubtadi's sequence through the standard fiqh topics. For each ruling in the primer, Al-Hidayah provides: the Hanafi school's evidential basis (Quranic verse, prophetic hadith, or the opinion of the school's founding imams); an explanation of the ruling's meaning and application; and, frequently, a comparison with the positions of other schools. This comparative dimension — engaging with the Shafi'i and Maliki schools in particular — makes Al-Hidayah not only a Hanafi legal guide but an introduction to comparative Islamic jurisprudence.

The evidential dimension of Al-Hidayah was innovative in its time. Earlier Hanafi manuals — including the works of ash-Shaybani from which Bidayat al-Mubtadi draws — typically stated rulings without their evidential basis, assuming that students would receive the evidence through oral teaching. Al-Hidayah incorporates the evidence directly into the text, making the school's positions more transparent and defensible in scholarly debate.

Al-Hidayah was translated into English by Charles Hamilton in 1791 — one of the earliest translations of an Islamic legal text into a European language — and this translation influenced the administration of Islamic law in British India for several decades. A more reliable modern translation was produced by Imran Nyazee in the twentieth century. These translations attest to the work's importance not only within the Islamic scholarly world but in the broader history of Islamic law's encounter with modernity.

The continuing centrality of Al-Hidayah in Hanafi legal education — from the madrasas of South Asia to the religious universities of Central Asia and Turkey — reflects its unique combination of authority, accessibility, and comparative breadth. Students who master Al-Hidayah have a comprehensive map of Hanafi law, a grounding in its evidential basis, and an awareness of how it relates to the positions of other schools.
content_id:
Al-Hidayah fi Sharh Bidayat al-Mubtadi (Panduan: Syarah atas Bidayat al-Mubtadi) karya Burhan ad-Din Ali ibn Abi Bakr al-Marghinani (511–593 H / 1135–1197 M) merupakan teks paling otoritatif dan paling banyak digunakan dalam mazhab Hanafi. Ditulis sebagai syarah atas kitab primer al-Marghinani sendiri, yaitu Bidayat al-Mubtadi, Al-Hidayah menyajikan penjelasan, dasar dalil, dan penalaran hukum yang menjadikan rumusan hukum ringkas dalam kitab primer tersebut dapat dipahami dan diterapkan.

Hubungan antara Al-Hidayah dan Bidayat al-Mubtadi bersifat unik dalam tradisi hukum Islam: satu ulama yang sama menulis baik kitab primer maupun syarahnya, sehingga syarah ini mencerminkan penafsiran yang otoritatif atas teks aslinya, bukan pembacaan ulama belakangan atas karya orang lain. Al-Marghinani memahami persis apa yang ia maksudkan dari setiap ketetapan hukum dalam Bidayat al-Mubtadi dan menjelaskannya dalam Al-Hidayah dengan otoritas sebagai pengarang aslinya.

Susunan Al-Hidayah mengikuti urutan Bidayat al-Mubtadi dalam membahas topik-topik fiqh yang baku. Untuk setiap ketetapan hukum dalam kitab primer, Al-Hidayah menyajikan: dasar dalil mazhab Hanafi (ayat Al-Qur'an, hadits Nabi, atau pendapat para imam pendiri mazhab); penjelasan makna dan penerapan ketetapan hukum tersebut; dan, seringkali, perbandingan dengan pendapat mazhab-mazhab lain. Dimensi komparatif ini — yang secara khusus mengkaji mazhab Syafi'i dan Maliki — menjadikan Al-Hidayah bukan sekadar panduan hukum Hanafi, melainkan juga pengantar dalam fiqh perbandingan Islam.

Dimensi dalil dalam Al-Hidayah merupakan inovasi pada masanya. Kitab-kitab panduan Hanafi sebelumnya — termasuk karya-karya asy-Syaybani yang menjadi sumber Bidayat al-Mubtadi — biasanya mencantumkan ketetapan hukum tanpa menyebutkan dasar dalilnya, dengan asumsi bahwa para murid akan menerima dalil tersebut melalui pengajaran lisan. Al-Hidayah memasukkan dalil secara langsung ke dalam teks, sehingga posisi mazhab menjadi lebih transparan dan dapat dipertahankan dalam perdebatan ilmiah.

Al-Hidayah diterjemahkan ke dalam bahasa Inggris oleh Charles Hamilton pada tahun 1791 — salah satu terjemahan paling awal teks hukum Islam ke dalam bahasa Eropa — dan terjemahan ini memengaruhi pelaksanaan hukum Islam di India Britania selama beberapa dekade. Terjemahan modern yang lebih andal dihasilkan oleh Imran Nyazee pada abad kedua puluh. Terjemahan-terjemahan ini membuktikan pentingnya karya ini tidak hanya dalam dunia ilmiah Islam, tetapi juga dalam sejarah yang lebih luas tentang perjumpaan hukum Islam dengan modernitas.

Sentralitas Al-Hidayah yang terus berlanjut dalam pendidikan hukum Hanafi — dari madrasah-madrasah di Asia Selatan hingga universitas-universitas keagamaan di Asia Tengah dan Turki — mencerminkan perpaduan uniknya antara otoritas, aksesibilitas, dan keluasan komparatif. Para pelajar yang menguasai Al-Hidayah memiliki peta komprehensif tentang hukum Hanafi, pemahaman mendalam tentang dasar dalilnya, serta kesadaran mengenai keterkaitannya dengan pendapat mazhab-mazhab lain.
title_id: Al-Hidayah Syarah Bidayat al-Mubtadi: Komentar Pintu Gerbang
content_ar: Al-Hidayah fi Sharh Bidayat al-Mubtadi (The الهداية: A Commentary on The Beginning for the Student) by Burhan ad-Din علي ibn Abi Bakr al-Marghinani (511–593 AH / 1135–1197 CE) is the most المعتمد and widely used text in the الحنفي school. Written as a commentary on al-Marghinani's own primer Bidayat al-Mubtadi, Al-Hidayah provides the explanations, evidential basis, and legal reasoning that make the primer's concise rulings intelligible and applicable. The relationship between Al-Hidayah and Bidayat al-Mubtadi is unique in the Islamic legal التراث: the same scholar wrote both the primer and its commentary, meaning that the commentary reflects an المعتمد interpretation of the original text rather than a later scholar's reading of another's work. Al-Marghinani knew exactly what he intended by each ruling in Bidayat al-Mubtadi and explains it in Al-Hidayah with the authority of the original author. Al-Hidayah's organization follows Bidayat al-Mubtadi's sequence through the standard الفقه topics. For each ruling in the primer, Al-Hidayah provides: the الحنفي school's evidential basis (القرآني الآية, prophetic الحديث, or the opinion of the school's founding imams); an explanation of the ruling's meaning and application; and, frequently, a comparison with the positions of other schools. This comparative dimension — engaging with the الشافعي and المالكي schools وبالأخص — makes Al-Hidayah not only a الحنفي legal guide but an introduction to comparative Islamic jurisprudence. The evidential dimension of Al-Hidayah was innovative in its time. Earlier الحنفي manuals — بما في ذلك the works of ash-Shaybani from which Bidayat al-Mubtadi draws — typically stated rulings without their evidential basis, assuming that students would receive the evidence through oral teaching. Al-Hidayah incorporates the evidence directly into the text, making the school's positions more transparent and defensible in العلمي debate. Al-Hidayah was translated into English by Charles Hamilton in 1791 — one of the earliest translations of an Islamic legal text into a European language — and this translation influenced the administration of Islamic law in British India for several decades. A more reliable modern translation was produced by Imran Nyazee in the twentieth century. These translations attest to the work's importance not only within the Islamic العلمي الدنيا but in the broader history of Islamic law's encounter with modernity. The continuing centrality of Al-Hidayah in الحنفي legal education — from the madrasas of South Asia to the religious universities of المحوري Asia and Turkey — reflects its unique combination of authority, accessibility, and comparative breadth. Students who master Al-Hidayah have a comprehensive map of الحنفي law, a grounding in its evidential basis, and an awareness of how it relates to the positions of other schools.
